prompt:
Scene:
A square commercial beauty-or-fashion image designed like a polished campaign proof for Beauty Look Era Switchboard. Keep the camera discipline clear and believable: portrait-oriented within the square, premium studio light, controlled tonal contrast, and enough surrounding negative space to feel like an ad layout without relying on readable typography. The image should look production-ready, with realistic skin, hair, fabric, and makeup rendering that supports identity locking instead of distracting from it.

Subject:
Build the frame around one clearly recognizable person. Lookbook Sheet should feel like a specific transformation step that a fashion or salon lookbook page using one consistent identity across multiple panels. The face structure, age, body proportions, and general camera position must stay consistent with the same-person premise of Transform one face across distinct beauty directions such as clean-girl, Y2K, runway editorial, bridal, and retro glamour while preserving recognizability.. Styling, wardrobe, grooming, makeup, hair, or pose can evolve, but the viewer must believe it is unquestionably the same subject across the whole theme. Use a premium editorial-commercial tone rather than a casual portrait session.

Important details:
The visual test is identity consistency under change. Preserve stable bone structure, eye spacing, nose shape, lip volume, skin tone family, and believable texture response. Let the transformation be visible through styling choices instead of facial drift. This theme studies editing-workflow, multi-image-reference, photorealism, commercial-mockup through a production-ready visual system. Make the composition clean enough for client review, with professional grooming fidelity and enough detail for salon, beauty, or fashion decision-making. Aim for clear hierarchy, polished craft, and immediately usable campaign logic.

Use case:
Beauty Look Era Switchboard single study 2/5.

Constraints:
No readable text, no watermark, no logos, preserve exact 1:1 aspect ratio, no face drift, no inconsistent age shift, no extra limbs, no duplicate person, no asymmetric eye placement, no beauty-editorial chaos.
title: Lookbook Sheet
description: Lookbook Sheet for Beauty Look Era Switchboard, a fashion or salon lookbook page using one consistent identity across multiple panels.

generation.runner_notes: Independent single-image study with no runtime reference dependency.
